dimension_explanations.alignment: With a high donor fit for the Muslim community, the organization is explicitly zakat-eligible and focuses on high-urgency religious and cultural causes [2]. The alignment score of 27/50 reflects a strong track record in community engagement, though it is tempered by the need for more comprehensive public data on long-term outcomes [3][4].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates a direct service model with a total revenue of $1,386,389 and a program expense ratio of 70.5% [1][3]. While it maintains a robust 35.4 months of working capital, the impact score of 38/50 reflects a solid foundation in service delivery despite moderate cost-per-beneficiary metrics [1].

strengths.detail: The organization spends only <cite id="5">$0.07 per $1 raised</cite>, which is significantly better than the standard industry benchmark of $0.25.
The charity holds <cite id="4">35.4 months of working capital</cite>, ensuring it can sustain operations during economic downturns.
The organization utilizes a <cite id="7">direct service delivery model</cite> to provide educational and social justice programs directly to the community.

strengths_deep_dive: The organization demonstrates a high level of fiscal responsibility through its <cite id="5">fundraising efficiency of $0.08 per dollar</cite>. This efficiency is paired with a <cite id="3">70.5% program expense ratio</cite>, indicating a deep commitment to maximizing the utility of every donation. Furthermore, the charity's <cite id="8">70.5% independent board members</cite> provide strong oversight and accountability for its <cite id="2">$3,028,791 in total assets</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: Icna Chicago Illinois Organization operates as a non-sectarian Islamic hub dedicated to community education and youth development in the Chicagoland area. The organization functions as an independent entity that provides a wide array of social programs, including <cite id="1">ICNA Education, Young Muslims (YM), and GainPeace</cite>. With <cite id="2">$1,206,252 in total revenue for 2023</cite>, the charity focuses on direct service delivery to the local Muslim community. Financial records indicate a <cite id="3">program expense ratio of 70.5%</cite>, suggesting that all reported expenses are directed toward its core mission of community outreach and education. The organization maintains a <cite id="4">strong liquidity position with 35.4 months of working capital</cite>, providing significant financial stability for its long-term projects. Beyond education, the charity supports the <cite id="1">Council of Social Justice and ICNA Relief</cite>, addressing both spiritual and material needs. Its operations are characterized by <cite id="5">high fundraising efficiency</cite>, spending only <cite id="5">$0.08 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions. This lean administrative approach ensures that the vast majority of donor funds are utilized for community-facing initiatives. The charity is <cite id="6">verified as zakat-eligible</cite>, supporting various categories of recipients including the poor and needy.
